questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Examine the following statements
I. I watch TV only if I am bored.
II. I am never bored when I have my brother's company.
III. Whenever I go to the theater I take my brother along.

Which one of the following conclusions is valid in the context of the above statements?

choices.a: If I am bored, I watch TV
choices.b: If I am bored, I seek my brother's company
choices.c: If I am not with my brother, then I watch TV
choices.d: If I am not bored, I do not watch TV

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Consider the following statements
I. All artists are whimsical.
II. Some artists are drug addicts.
III. Frustrated people are prone to become drug addicts

From the above three statements it may concluded that

choices.c: all frustrated people are drug addicts

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Consider the following three statements
I. Only students can participate in the race.
II. Some participants in the race are girls.
III. All participants in the race are invited for coaching.

Which one of the following conclusions can be drawn from the above statements?

choices.a: All participants in the race are invited for coaching
choices.b: All students are invited for coaching
choices.c: All participants in the race are students

solutions.solution: (d) From both the statements it is clear that only Ravi has five pens in the class. Therefore, only Conclusion (d) follows.
(d) Without hard word, one does not pass can be concluded.
(b) This is a good book can be concluded.
(d) Only Conclusion I follows.
(c) If a player is found guilty of doping, his medal is confiscated. Therefore, only Conclusion II follows.
(c) From both the statements it is clear that Conclusion I is true.
(d) Neither Conclusion I nor II follows. Overpopulation is a result of several factors. Therefore, only unscientific thinking cannot be held responsible for overpopulation.
(b) The second statement clearly corroborates the Conclusion II.
(d) From the statements it is clear that knowledge is interdisciplinary. Thus, Conclusion I follows.

(b) Only those who have binoculars can be a part of birdwatcher's club, hence all the members of birdwatcher's club will have binoculars for sure. But all those who have binoculars may not be part of birdwatcher's club.

(d) Anand comes on a bicycle which is not used by any teacher. Hence, Anand cannot be a teacher. Therefore, I is valid. II is not valid because if Anand is not a teacher, he is either a student or a staff member.
(d) Presence of word 'most' in the statement makes both I and II ambiguous. Hence, none of I and II can be drawn.
(b) I does not make any connection with the given statement but II does. Hence, I cannot be drawn but II can be. Fruits —→ B-Complex —→ Good for health
(b) I cannot be concluded because of the presence of word 'all' in it. II is the obvious choice from both the statements.
(d) I is invalid as export is not the subject of discussion in the given statement. II is a valid conclusion because there has been increase in production of two wheelers with improved qualities.
(b) Most dresses are expensive means some are cheap also.

(b) If all the students in a particular class are intelligent and Rashmi is not intelligent, then it means Rashmi is not the student of that particular class which does not have a single student who is not intelligent.
(b) From the above two statements, it is not clear whether the parliament can assure the fundamental rights or not.
(c) Option (c) is required to validate the conclusion stated in the given passage.

(c) No reasons are specified in the question for going to different camps. As, Ankit has been going to different camps since the past two year, so it can be said that he goes to some type of camp every summer.

(c) No reason is provided for discontinuation of any programme. Hence, option (d) is not correct. Three shows were discontinued. As no sitcoms shows were discontinued, they must be either drama or news magazine. Now, there were only two news magazines. So, atleast one discontinued show was a drama. Hence, option (c) is correct.
(b) It is given that all married persons are invited for the dance and some members of the club are married persons. So, all married students of the club are invited for the dance.
(d) The pre-condition for watching TV is to get bored. So, If I am not bored, I do not watch TV. So, option (d) is correct.
(b) All artists are whimsical and some of the artists are drug addicts. Thus, all those artists who are drug addicts are also whimsical. Hence, some drug addicts are whimsical.

(c) From Statement III, it can be concluded that 'All participants in the race are students'. Since, it is given in Statement III that 'All girl participants in the race are invited for coaching'. So options (a) and (b) cannot be concluded. Hence, answer is option (c).
